Sired by Brigadier-General Charles Tenley (active), a Gangrel who collects tourists worldwide and uses them; few neophytes survive past that stage. Tegan fears but respects him and believes she needs him to survive.
Lured by a neophyte to the Brigadier's villa, fed on and Embraced because he lacked an Australian. Embraced by choice: turn or die.
Embraced into a 'pack' of the Brigadier's neophytes who shared the same sire and the same rivalry.

Spent early nights in Mumbai, where the Brigadier holds a fiercely protected territory with a haven hidden on the wooded coast; he does not participate in the Mumbai court but sends neophytes into the city.

Father, mother and younger sister (now 48) are alive in Tasmania. No contact since 2000; Tegan remains on the Tasmanian missing persons list and stalks them on social media, knowing revealing herself would breach the Masquerade.
The smell of pine, recalling exploring the forests with her sister and father as a child.

Preys on inebriated people, luring drunks to 'a new place where the bar won't cut you off' or feeding on the high at raves, clubs and parties. Prefers the city, dark alleys and busy streets. Cannot feed on cows.

First kill was a loss of control: banished from the haven and surviving in the forest, she came across an unexplained kine, killed them in hunger and anger without meaning to, and buried the body the same night.
Sent to Sydney by her sire to gather intelligence for him: who is most likely to become Prince, who has the Prince's ear, their weaknesses, and whether the Australian court is worth the risk.

To establish territory for her sire and herself and subtly destabilise the court so no one gains a steady foothold; intends to stay in Sydney as it is easier to blend in while still a missing person in Tasmania.
Use her low covenant status to fly under the radar while enjoying the protection of a strong covenant; aspires to indirect influence as a socialite.
Embraces her Gangrel heritage but is wary of elders; her main goal is to learn to shift into animals like her sire.

Sees the Traditions as beneficial and necessary but only effective where people can be held accountable. Appreciates Elysium but feels it is currently just a fragile promise not to attack, with no sacred Kindred places or real fear of consequences in Australia.
Followed the Lancea because her sire is Lancea and never considered she had a choice; privately would have chosen the Crone and is now finding the Brigadier's teachings differ from her covenant's.
Views mortals as food, entertainment and replaceable; pities ghouls and treats them politely.
Knows she should oppose the Circle of the Crone but is secretly very curious about them.

Sired by Gwylim Lloyd (destroyed), who Embraced Edna to be eternal companion to her husband, his intended new Paladin; the husband did not survive the change. Edna felt freed by Lloyd's destruction, regards him as an embarrassment, and mentions him only when formally required.
Embraced in a violent attack while accompanying her late husband, a Mission President, in Ohio; her husband did not survive the Embrace.
Embraced in Cincinnati, Ohio.

Spent her early nights performing ritualistic sin-eating for her sire and fellow clergy beneath Old St. Mary's Church; confined to the grounds and tended by a kind Kindred Sister, she began to lose her grip on her sanity.
Last resided in New Orleans, under contested Carthian rule, currently unstable and hotly contested by the Lance and Invictus.
Was Mormon before her Embrace; her sire framed her Mormonism as heresy requiring penance, recruiting her as a vampiric ritual sin-eater for the church.
Watches a living descendant, her great-great-niece Ellen, out of fascination; Ellen has left the LDS Church, divorced, and lives into her 60s drinking wine and eating beignets.

Her husband's remains, which she has moved with her everywhere and tends regularly (seasons, his birthday, their anniversary); she chats to him and pretends, while privately glad he is dead and unable to see what she has become.
Her own copy of The Book of Mormon, tied to the unhealed wound of being punished for her faith on entering the Lancea Sanctum.

Cultivates a willing herd (cult) she treats as well-loved livestock, feeding them, preaching to them, washing and grooming them and keeping them healthy, mirroring how Sister Agnes maintained her in Ohio.
Came to Sydney seeking power for its own sake and a second attempt at 1970s American Evangelism done 'the right way'; views Sydney as belonging to her by right of God.
To establish territory and gather as large a flock as she can; aspires to the role of Bishop.
Outwardly to be seen as an intellectual and humble caregiver worthy of respect; inwardly to have the church unwittingly increase her own power and restore the city to the way God intended.
Sees the Masquerade as necessary; resents the Progeny tradition, believing she should be allowed to Embrace as she can see who God needs; views Amaranth as outdated but has no desire for it.
Honours Elysium as a civilised necessity and disdains using Nightmare merely to get her way as lazy. Views mortals primarily as food, useful when they worship Him.
Holds particular disgust for the Carthians, regarding them as all pretense and incapable of the control needed to avoid hunters.
Had a non-confrontational working relationship with Kirk Grimm during the rise of US evangelism in the 1970s, both playing their roles well (to be confirmed).

Her faith in the Mormon God was never healed after being punished for it on entering the Lance, and she secretly suspects God does not exist except within herself, treating that thought as heresy and burying it even from herself.
Sired by Dr Thomas Flanders (active), his mentor, a forensic pathologist and mortician who worked alongside Conrad and gave him a thorough, harsh education before the Embrace.

On the brink of death after confronting the man who would be his sire, Conrad (then a police detective) was Embraced; his sire heard voices he interpreted as the Holy Ghost demanding it and resisted serving penance.
Was a police detective in mortal life.
Spent his early nights in London, a traditional Kindred city ruled by the elite Invictus and seconded by the Sanctified.
Has a living granddaughter who moved to Sydney some years ago and greatly resembles his late wife; a significant reason he was keen to leave London.
His granddaughter in Sydney, who resembles his late wife.
Stalks and ambushes his prey.
Brought to Sydney on a holy mission from his superiors to bring the word of Longinus to the new Australian frontier; attends Court out of inquisitorial duty and loyalty to his covenant.

Hopes to see the Lancea et Sanctum rule Sydney, induct more nameless Kindred, and severely diminish the Circle's witches; aspires to become Sheriff or Inquisitor General to expand his inquisitorial power.

Incredibly conservative in his adherence to the Book of Longinus and intolerant of anyone straying from it, especially within his own covenant; aims to maintain ideological purity and extinguish blasphemy. Holds the three Traditions as commandments never to be broken.

Enjoys the civility of Elysium, which suits his methods of friendly discussion and covert interrogation given little aptitude for violence. Sees himself as a sheepdog over the Kine, invested in humanity's moral and spiritual health.

Allied with Carver (Archer W), a former Crone turned antiquarian whose apprehension and redemption into the Lance Conrad oversaw; Conrad sees Carver as proof any Kindred can find purpose with the Lance.

Long-standing partner of Kirk Grimm (Phil G), a Crimson Cavalry member who turned on his sire to help expel them from London; the two solve mysteries together and were sent to Australia as a professional duo.
Sympathises with a Kindred (Alana W) whose sire was a colonial relic in India and who suffered an improper Embrace and education; Conrad feels a need to rectify the abuse they suffered.

Enemy of Charlie Ballsack (Kurtis C), a mercenary whose run-ins with Conrad have ended poorly: kidnapping a Kindred targeted for questioning, slaying one of Conrad's friends used as hunter bait, and collecting a bounty on a man who faked repentance to escape persecution.
Despises the Circle of the Crone as servants of the Devil and slaves to the Beast, and dislikes the Carthian Movement given his privileged upbringing.

Enemy of Rene (Luca V), a Carthian upstart; their proxy war via protests and police peaked when St Leon's church in London burnt down in a riot, killing Kindred in its basement, with each blaming the other.

facts.note: Counterparty is the grandsire who perpetrated the act; Conrad's resentment and suspicion are the hidden element.
facts.value: His grandsire Keeper / Mnemosyne (Symon G) is his secret stalker who, on learning of Conrad, ate Conrad's memories from his wife's mind, lobotomising her. Conrad suspects this and harbours deep resentment beneath an outwardly friendly relationship but has not acted.
PI Office - 101 Rookwood Rd, Yagoona. Appartment - 120 Flinders St, Darlinghurst

Sired by Helena De Witt (never onscreen), who saw Cyrus as a means to keep her global network of favours, deals, double-crosses and takeovers ticking along; his person and his events provide cover for her moves.
Embraced through a ritual of mutual blood-letting held within Helena's coven.
Embraced in Los Angeles; spent his first years of unlife on the West Coast before national outings as Helena's messenger, kept on a short leash.
A DJ who ran cult-like musical gatherings of drug-fuelled ecstatic dance; holds a barely-remembered BA in Ancient History.

Last spent more than a month in London, ruled by a cabal of Ventrue Invictus from the financial district; he kept to lifestyle districts like Soho and survived one attempt on his unlife by an old enemy of Helena's, foiled by a ghoul.
Had older parents in middle America, dead by the time Helena let him leave LA; Cyrus donates to their favourite charities without her knowledge.
Keeps two ghouls who act as his tech team and, unbeknownst to him, occasional babysitters ensuring his hedonism does not derail Helena's plans.
The music of his parents, particularly the Beatles.
Feeds trivially at club events, festivals and raves, exploiting what people will do to get backstage and how carried away they become once there.
First kill was at a full-moon party in Thailand; he threw the body into the ocean and later learned she was underage, with her parents in the news for weeks. 'Never again.'
Kept abreast of Sydney's clan and covenant jockeying for his sire Helena, weaving her specific desires for the city into broader Daeva and Crone plans; she will be very upset if he fails.
Tasked by Helena to create a welcoming corner of the city (plus bolt-holes) for visiting Crones, establishing a beachhead with good views and access to the kine; aspires to be a socialite.

Believes the Crones have a marketing problem and wants to connect people to the Goddess via a well-timed bassline drop rather than shadowy gatherings; sees DJing as creating shared, seductive moments superior to the Daeva's narrow approach.

Holds the Masquerade easy to keep ('hunt where people won't believe what they've seen'); thinks sire and childe should be punished equally for the whelp's sins and that Amaranth is untenable. Fond of mortals as an honest audience.
Despises the Invictus ('scheming gits' who treat eternity like a profit and loss statement).
Allied with Brandy (CoC): he gives her ladies from the DDs unforgettable weekends at huge events like EDC, and she speeds permits for his events through city planning.
Has a mutual-recognition rapport with Einar Solvieg (Carthian); they have crossed paths on at least three continents and will go back-to-back if things go wrong.
He and Brandy probably owe each other roughly one to two boons apiece.
Lyn's character has seen Cyrus at Kindred events where horrible things have happened; whether Cyrus actually knew is left open.
May Street, St Peters

Sired by Elliot Beaux (missing), a colleague rather than a friend, who Embraced Cazz hoping to share his nights; the Embrace surfaced a shared derangement, and Elliot abandoned Cazz years later. Cazz still keeps an ear out for him, just in case.
Embraced unexpectedly one late night in France when research ran late; Cazz knew of their sire but they were colleagues at best.
Embraced in Paris, France, where Cazz was studying and working and first encountered the Carthian Movement.
A university researcher with little social life; never finished their studies after the Embrace, continuing to work until distracted research and lack of aging risked the Masquerade.
The University of Paris (the section now Paris-East Creteil University), a reminder of what they once strove for in life and learning; they always return there.
Students at the university over the years, followed from afar as they achieve, fail and move on through their short lives; an ever-renewing constant, though Cazz often does not learn their names.

Drawn to Sydney and Court by pure curiosity: the chance to watch Kindred society built from the ground up, with a fresh territory to discover, take apart and reassemble, and the opportunity to sow a few seeds and see what happens.

To establish new, safe territory in a brand-new place, then see where things go; no initial drive to climb political ladders though they accept it may become a necessary evil. Sees themselves as a loyal-as-needed outlier guarding and trading secrets.
Joined the Carthians because other covenants subsume individuality in unmoving ideals; values that Carthians at least accept each other's existence and listen to the individual.

Respects laws that exist for tested reasons and would not break them as a first response, but they are not off the table given a very good reason. Follows Elysium's rules but is curious to find others' breaking point. Sees mortals as fascinating, loyal tools to be looked after.
Dislikes the social climbing Ventrue are known for, attributing it to their bloodline or mortal days as a researcher; still cultivates other Ventrue as needed.

Mutually beneficial relationship with Brendan, a Mekhet: Cazz acts as the jingling keys distracting others while Brendan investigates, and gains information from the situation and the other vampire in return.
Aylesbury St (Newtown)

Sired by Colton Beauregard Price (active), an Invictus Gangrel of a Daimonion lineage, who Embraced her as an act of calculated revenge and political cruelty, intending her transformation as a living punishment inflicted on another. He treats her as a failed instrument that survived its purpose; she rejects his claim and expects he will one night come to reclaim her.

Her Embrace was cold and deliberate, performed with perfect manners. She was taken to her sire's Texas torture ranch, drained slowly, and forced to endure every moment of fear with no frenzy or passion, only calculated intent. She rose in shock, hunger and animal panic.

After surviving an Embrace meant to destroy her, she was found by the Circle of the Crone in the Louisiana bayou, who interpreted her suffering as transformation rather than failure. Refuge became genuine belief; she is a member of the Daughters of the Goddess faction.

Spiritually reborn in the Louisiana bayou, where the Circle of the Crone found her. She knew a domain shaped by ancient presence and quiet spiritual authority rather than formal court rule, where power was diffuse and rooted in territory, ritual and survival.
Seeks the utter obliteration of one specific Gangrel lineage: her own.

Seeks understanding, mastery and spiritual strength within the Circle of the Crone, hoping in time to become a trusted ritualist or spiritual vessel who guides others through suffering into transformation. Driven by personal sovereignty rather than status or political power.
Came to Sydney via her Hierophant, who provided the opportunity to prove herself and to move her out of harm's way from her cruel sire. She hopes to infuriate her Invictus sire and make Mother proud.

Sees traditions and laws as necessary structures rather than sacred truths, respecting rules that preserve stability and protect the vulnerable but distrusting hierarchy presented as holy. Respects the Masquerade and Elysium as necessary containment, while recognising Elysium's civility as a performance masking power.

Deeply distrustful of the Invictus, whose obsession with hierarchy, ownership and perfected civility mirrors her sire's philosophy. She does not seek open conflict but quietly resists their influence wherever it reinforces ownership over autonomy.

Feeds primarily from a small devotional cult that gathers around her as a spiritual vessel of transformation, taking blood through ritualised, controlled, consensual feeding. Outside the cult she hunts rarely, following Gangrel instincts: patient, silent and deliberate, in isolated places, ending the hunt quickly and cleanly.

Her first kill was a sanctioned ritual hunt rather than an accident: under a blood moon she ran with a Circle pack in a sacred frenzy marking her passage into the covenant, and the prey did not survive.

Keeps a small alligator in a reinforced tank of mud and dark water from the Louisiana bayou, representing patient, controlled predation. Also keeps a weathered trailer on the industrial fringe of Sydney modified to resemble the one she grew up in, the only place she allows herself to exist without performance.

Some of her mortal family are still alive in the American South. Officially she is a quiet disappearance with no body found; they believe she ran off or met a bad end. She keeps her distance out of protection, refusing to let them become leverage.
Indulgences include motivational speaking, occult worship, fanatic orgies, whiskey tastings, opera and demolition derbies.
Sired by Abigail Mason, wealthy and respected, who saw potential in Reed and appreciated his drive. He took the chance to come to Sydney to get out from under her thumb.
Taken late after a business meeting, believing he was getting into a good deal and not realising it would cost his life. He is not disappointed with the result.
Embraced in New York and spent his early years as a Kindred there, in a turbulent political landscape of Kindred acting against each other but with a strong Ventrue and Invictus group.
Came to Sydney for opportunity and to escape his sire's control. He intends to take the city over, or rip out its guts to extract value before leaving.
Pursues advancement and power, aspiring to roles such as Prince, Primogen or high socialite. Wants to use the skills of others to protect him while he forwards his own personal goals.

Views the Traditions as useful only insofar as they further his motives, especially using others' breaches to gain social power. Revels in the sanctity of Elysium where physical threats are lessened, and regards mortals as resources, food and numbers in an equation.
Opposes the Circle of the Crone and the Lancea et Sanctum, finding their prattling tedious; wants to rile them up against each other.
Has some mortal family, though most have long disassociated from him over differences in ethics. He still sends money to and calls his elderly mother.
Hunts using manipulation and isolation, getting people alone and then dominating them.
His first kill was a man who had followed him from a bar, angry that Reed had taken his girlfriend from him on a previous night out.
Indulgences include shopping, whiskey, and expensive things he doesn't use.
The Underground, 424-426 Harris Street Pyrmont

A failed adult film director and failed film director generally. He dreamed of becoming the next Spielberg but was reduced to commercials and ultimately adult films, doing well during the golden age of the adult film industry (1977 to 1984) before low-quality video took over.

His sire is active. Jack first thought he was turned as an act of sadism, but later learned that his sire and others in the Moulding Room had followed his mortal life for years, quietly interfering and obstructing his opportunities to lead him down a path of failure. He was a cultivated asset, now turned loose in Sydney.
His sire found him standing on a building ledge ready to end it all, listened to his story, then Embraced him, depriving him of his agency one more time. It was violent, unwanted and resented.

His early years were in Los Angeles, filled with violence and debauchery where Kine and weaker Kindred were playthings. LA had a very stable Praxis; his sire and covenant thrived around the edges, supporting the Prince with dirt on everyone in exchange for freedom from oversight.
Sees Sydney as a stepping stone to gain power and prove a point: that the Kine are as monstrous as Kindred and just need to be shown the way.

Seeks secrets of greater power and truths, and advancement within the Circle of the Crone. Aspires to be a socialite, using his skills to build power and influence; he is impatient and wants to achieve goals sooner.

Abides by the Traditions hoping that one night Kindred will no longer be bound by any of them. Always honours Elysium because someone is always watching. Regards mortals as playthings and test subjects.
Opposes the Lancea et Sanctum; he had little time for priests as a mortal and sees no need for them now, having learned deeper Crone truths that go beyond the Lance.

A former heavy cocaine user, he especially likes to feed off Kine who are high. He holds and participates in raucous drug-fuelled orgies and always offers narcotics to his intended victims. He will not work with or feed on animals or children.

His first kill was prepared for him by his sire: a victim drunk and stoned and helpless while Jack was starving. He is haunted by her face and now always blindfolds his victims in case of over-feeding, to avoid more ghosts.

Classy, old, vintage cinemas ground him; cinema is the only way he can still experience human emotion. He attends late-night showings at the Dendy or Orpheum, sitting alone in the corner and losing himself in the experience.
Relatives are probably alive, but he has had no contact since his Embrace.
Indulgences are sex, drugs and cinema.

His very secret sponsor in the Moulding Room tracks up the chain to leadership in Palantir, which is in fact controlled by Kindred behind the scenes. He has been given free rein to treat Sydney as a petri dish and control group to test mortal limits of horror and perversion using the power of surveillance, and to report back on his findings.

I moved my previous haven because Ballzac had put a tracker on me.  It is now beneath floorboards in a storage room of the abandoned Hub Theatre in Newtown (where I had held court...I am fond of that place).. I had made copies of the keys when they were loaned to me for Court.

Her sire is missing: someone Alice met online discussing conspiracy theories, obsessed with finding a greater truth and needing someone with technological aptitude to deep-dive for them. Before disappearing shortly after her Embrace, the sire grew increasingly paranoid, claiming to be close to a big truth. Alice never learned whether the sire was disappeared for knowing too much or was simply delusional.

Embraced at a New Year's Eve party around the turn of 2000, while talking about Y2K and the end of the world with someone she had met on a BBS forum. The person asked if she wanted a chance to discover more about the truth of the world; she said yes, expecting conspiracy material rather than vampires.
Embraced in Sydney during New Year's Eve approaching the year 2000.

Wants knowledge and a foothold, to know what is going on and whether it can be used to get more information. Her sire's disappearance may be because the sire found out forbidden information, or because the sire was simply insane.
Seeks secrets and to discover what happened to her sire, or whether the sire was delusional. Leaning toward the Circle of the Crone, the covenant her sire belonged to.

Thinks the Traditions are sensible but does not know much of their finer details or history. Honours Elysium for now, but might change her view if she sees others break it and get away with it. Still quite close to mortals and very uncomfortable around ghouls because of the slavery parallels.

After trying and panicking at the blood-bank route, she went to Kings Cross and fed on men preying on women too drunk to consent. Nowadays she hunts down people who post awful things online and eats them.
Her first kill came when she frenzied while hunting in Kings Cross; she then panicked and hid the corpse on a rooftop.
Her family is currently her main touchstone: her parents and her sister. She also has connections to her local library and to the online forums she still frequents.
Her relatives are still alive and do not know she is dead; she emails them often in the middle of the night.
Indulgences are books and knowledge, and arguing on the internet.
Embraced in his forties into the Order of Sir Martin, then inducted into the Invictus within only weeks. He is bound by unbreakable oath to the Order and therefore to his duty to the First Estate.
A member of the Order of Sir Martin, a knightly order within the Invictus. He exists slightly lateral to the ordinary covenant hierarchy, bound by oath to the Order.
Came to Sydney under orders to keep the First Estate pure in the badlands. He attends Court to keep watch over his covenant mates and ensure those who break their vows are dealt with accordingly.

Hopes to become an integral part of the murkier parts of the Sydney underworld, rebuild standing among criminal-adjacent organisations and find the centre of the web. Within the covenant he aspires to Enforcer; in the City proper, to be beneath notice. His goals revolve around cultivating fear and informants.

Holds the Traditions vital and breaches to be dealt with swiftly and severely, while accepting he must ignore them himself: he sins so that others do not have to. Follows Elysium always to the letter, never the spirit. Tolerates no ghouls, and ignores anything outside the First Estate that is not a tool or obstacle.

Sired by Grace Gardener (active), known as 'the Gardener', leader of a satanic cult masquerading as a Pentecostal commune in the American Bible Belt (Texas or Alabama). She kept her progeny intentionally weaker than herself; Charles played at whatever she wanted to rise in her favour, then arranged to be sent to Sydney. He hates her and has never forgiven the deception and manipulation of his faith, despite still using it to feed.

Recruited as a human into an American Pentecostal cult, actually a satanic faith blending the Mother and other Crone lore. He served as a loyal human before being initiated into the inner circle and Embraced in the 2010s.

Embraced during a cultish ceremony of initiation into the cult's 'true' faith, observed by other blood-siblings and ghouls. He then served his sire, tending the flock, while adored by his wife, who was made a ghoul shortly after their son was born.

Early nights spent in the American South, at a cult commune outside a major city in Texas or Alabama. His last city was a Lance and Invictus stronghold with conservative, authoritarian politics, where the Crone had to be polite and meek to avoid being crushed.

In America he was an underling of his sire and had been plotting her demise and a hostile takeover of the cult. Sydney is an opportunity to escape her control and carve his own legacy by centring a new cult around himself.

Wants authority and control, pursuing Primogen and a high socialite role. Seeks respect as a clan figure, having been badly emasculated by his sire and harbouring deep resentment and hidden insecurity about it.

Holds the Traditions important for practical function, though thinks a few new ones should be added now and then. Not a violent person; appreciates Elysium safe-zones and views those who resort to violence as unintelligent. Sees mortals as tools and pets, potentially beloved but ultimately less than vampires; acknowledges he is a hypocrite.
Cultivation is his major technique: he primarily feeds on the faithful of his cult, with select outings and visits where a little charm and Dominate come in handy.

Early after his Embrace he and another Gorgon Embraced at a similar time killed someone together while out hunting. Both were punished by his sire Grace and required to publicly kneel and ask forgiveness from major Court figures. He never returned to that Court; Sydney is a fresh start so he is never publicly shamed again.

His major touchstone is his family. He met his ghouled wife in the cult they both belonged to; she supports his vampiric change. His main connection to humanity is his teenage son Ezekiel, whom he failed to turn into a ghoul around age 12 and who has since been conditioned via Dominate not to speak of his vampiric father but otherwise retains free human will. Now 16, Zeke attends an expensive Sydney boarding school and resents his father.

Brought his ghouled wife and teenage son Ezekiel to Sydney. His wife tends his cult during the day; his son Ezekiel, now 16, has been sent to an expensive Sydney boarding school to keep him distant from the fallout of his father's political schemes.

As is habitual to the Gorgons, he works with clay and stone, participating in night-only artisanal markets, making ritual items, talismans and artwork for auction. His rural estate includes a workshop, kiln and wild clay deposits. He refuses to garden; his grounds are unkept and infested with snakes.
French by birth; her mortal life and family were in France.

Sired by Oscar, a Daeva Carthian now in London who used 'The Wish' to entice her Embrace; he treated her essentially as his secretary and still occasionally demands her obeisance through well-timed leverage. She dislikes him.
Was involved in industrial activism in London before her Embrace.
Member of the Anti-Obstructionist Army (AOA), the radical wing of the Carthian Movement; embraced in London during a great Carthian-Lancea conflict and sent to Sydney by AOA contacts there.
Promised great power by Oscar and accepted the Embrace at his luxury estate; she got almost none of what was promised and grew bitter, eventually taking up the AOA's cause.
Embraced in London amid conflict between Carthians and the Lancea et Sanctum.
Had family in France; on joining the AOA she ritually severed ties, ensuring the mortal René was known to be dead.

Three touchstones: an elderly pen-pal who knows her only by a pen name and whom she has begun neglecting; an exploitative apartment-complex landlord she humanly hates; and Peter Rhone, a dockworker labouring under an exploitative oil-company contract whom she loves like a brother and joins at his bar.
Cultivates faux-trysts and seduction, feeding quickly and fleeing before her victims wake; often poaches other territories and avoids learning anything about her meals to prevent attachment.
Her first kill was a civilian her sire sent her to intimidate; the woman's obstinacy drove René to a frenzy and she killed her. Oscar was unconcerned, perhaps pleased.

Believes Sydney is a gift where mortals can explore ideas free of Kindred meddling; hopes ideally for the cessation of all Kindred interference, or at least advancement of the Anti-Obstructionist cause, even at the cost of her life.
Wishes to drag the Carthian Movement towards AOA radicalism and ideally hold an Enforcer office such as Sheriff or Hound to advance her goals.

Reveres the Masquerade as protecting human society from would-be vampire god-kings, but is unfussed about the other Traditions. Despises ghouling as a covenant practice (tolerated only out of practicality) and values mortals for innovation, not just vitae.

Closest ally is Einar Solveig, her coterie-mate and fellow Carthian, whom she was also sent to keep an eye on. Also allied with Eve (whom she inducted into the Movement), and with Doc and Macheath, fellow revolutionaries aware of her AOA sympathies who have helped her in the past.
Associates with Charlie Ballsack in dour business; respects and slightly fears him. He is aware of her more heinous acts and has witnessed her wavering commitment to the Carthian cause.
Conrad Sondergaard, a policeman she clashed with in London; mutual dislike grew into personal hatred over decades. Of all the London Lancea, Conrad is the most personal.
Keeper, a meddler in mortal practices; they have feuded ever since she trashed a cult hall of his, and he destroyed an artistic movement she was fond of.
Opposes the Lancea et Sanctum, her main rival, largely out of habit from fighting them in London.

Owes Cyrus Reynolds a major boon, inherited from her sire (who owed it to Cyrus's sire). She is bound to do a favour for whoever presents the promise and proof, though she does not know the holder is Cyrus.
Owes Doc and Macheath a major boon each for rescuing her and nursing her back to health in Ireland after a conflict with a ghoul.
Owes Charlie Ballsack a major boon for stepping in to do a job she pleaded for help with.
Eve likely owes René a minor boon for bringing her into the Movement (unconfirmed).
Her membership in the Anti-Obstructionist Army (AOA) is her most dangerous secret. Known to Doc, Macheath, Charlie Ballsack, Keeper, and Anichka.
Birkenhead Marina, 5 Cary St, Drummoyne, Bay A 17.

Has no identifiable sire; a posthumous, accidental Embrace carrying the blood of the First City, fused from the mingled vitae of two duelling Scions. She was taken on as ward and childe by the Nosferatu Charlie of the Order of Sir Martin, who trains, protects and mentors her.

An accidental, posthumous Embrace. Murdered by her pimp and dumped in a sewage shaft near a disused cemetery, her corpse was soaked in the mingled vitae of two Scions of the First City duelling above; she clawed her way out of an unmarked pauper's grave three nights later, feral and starved.
In mortal life she was a trafficked sex worker, sold on by her family when young and kept drugged and compliant by her owner.
Murdered by her owner (her pimp) in the back of a van after a client refused to pay, having outlived her usefulness; she was disposed of in a sewage shaft.

Drawn to the Circle of the Crone after the darkness of the sewers seemed to answer her pleas; displayed an innate affinity for Cruac without grasping its meaning. Charlie arranged ongoing mentoring from a contact she calls 'Scrambles' or 'Keeper' (Symon).

Her first nights were feral and solitary, hiding in the sewers, feeding on an injured rat by instinct, before Court ghouls dragged her into the light. A tribunal ruled her an illegal, unstable childe and ordered her destruction until Charlie intervened.
First awoke as Kindred in a city ruled by an unusual tribunal Praxis: two ancient rivals shared power as two judges, with the third seat traditionally held by the neutral Order of Sir Martin.
Has no living family she can turn to; her family sold her when young. She believes anyone who knew her thinks she is gone and has no desire to contact them.

An old homeless woman named 'Lady Di - the people's princess'. After Lady Di passed out drunk in her lap one night, Charlie followed Ivana, found the woman, and Ivana used Keeper to make them both forget her.
Treats mortals pragmatically as tools, threats, or sustenance; rarely intervenes except quiet, careful moments to protect a child or someone downtrodden.
Attends Court and remains in Sydney primarily because Charlie does; believes she owes him a life debt for saving her from destruction and feels unsafe being absent from where his obligations gather.
Wants only to survive, stay useful, and avoid being a liability or embarrassment; quietly hopes to begin claiming an identity that is her own rather than borrowed.

Sees the Traditions and Elysium as pragmatic survival rules rather than moral imperatives, having noted the hypocrisy of Kindred who bend them; follows them to stay alive and avoid bringing shame on Charlie. Believes herself the lowest of the low for being a posthumous, unauthorised Embrace, and feels weak for being a Gangrel without Protean.
Allies include Charlie, Scrambles/Keeper, Anichka, Doc, and Cyrus.
While human, Ivana unknowingly crossed paths with several Kindred in ways that could prove problematic for them when they realise who she is; she names René (Luca), Cyrus, and Skinner.
Grew up in Belfast between the declaration of the Irish State and the Troubles.

Sired by Karoline Lenya, herself a young vampire at the time, German and working-class. She gave him the name 'Macheath' to protect his mortal family, described the Norvegi bloodline as a tool of revolutionary power, and forged Carthian-mortal weapon pipelines. She entered torpor in the 1990s to escape enemies after diablerising an Invictus elder, just before the Good Friday Accords.

An Irish republican from boyhood; has been fighting the British in one way or another since British soldiers crippled his father when he was twelve. The Carthian Movement offered him the same promise as the IRA.

A true-believer Carthian who does not fit any faction cleanly; resembles the Saboteurs but has anti-overlap with the AOA, seeing involvement in mortal affairs as a duty rather than something to reject. Spent the last two decades travelling, starting fires and seeding resistance wherever he and Maggie went.
A practical Embrace: his sire drained him to a husk, fed him her blood, buried him three feet down in a deep cellar, and returned the next night to watch him dig his way out.
Belfast, at the start of the Troubles, a city at war by day and night.
Avoided his mortal family for decades to keep them safe; after his mother's death he discovered a branch had migrated to Australia and now keeps tabs on them.

His niece Fiona, matriarch of the Australian branch of the family, who has never met him and is the spitting image of his mother at the same age. He watches her clan from a distance; only Maggie knows.

Feeds on animals and discarded blood wherever possible (cleaning abattoir floors, boxing rings, surgical suites), leans on the Movement for access to other Carthians' herds, or feeds on mortals he is already targeting. His Norvegi heritage makes non-violent feeding almost impossible, and a drained victim passes as mummified.

His first kill was in mortal life. The first time his Beast killed during feeding, he came to himself holding a drained, desiccated corpse, the bony spines retracting into his hands; he is fairly sure it was an Orangeman enemy.

After years of travel, fighting and smuggling, the lure of land without existing tyrannies and hierarchies reignited a hope that had died with the end of armed struggle for a united Ireland; he sees Australia as a possible new frontier for that dream.

Wants to smash the ladder and reject traditional vampiric hierarchies: anarchy, liberation, freedom whether they like it or not. More of an enforcer, his main goal is tearing down those who set themselves above the rest.

Views the Traditions and Elysium as merely practical rules deserving no special reverence, and will exploit or break them when warranted. Comfortable with extreme violence in service of his principles; still feels close to humanity and gets involved in mortal affairs more than most Kindred would.

Maggie (Doc) is his closest companion and only coterie-mate of long standing, bound by ideology and then long-term trust. He defaults to basic trust with other Carthians and has worked with Einar, René Meyer and Eve enough to know their character.

Charlie Ballsack is probably the only Invictus he does not immediately want to throw into a furnace, since Charlie seems uncommitted to the First Estate and Mac feels Nosferatu are fellow travellers; they have crossed paths without killing each other.
Met Ivana before she was Embraced; she does not remember him but he feels obligated to keep an eye on her.

Kirk Grimm, Paladin of the Lance. They clashed by chance in a chaotic back-alley street fight in Belfast, both on the edge of frenzy with horrific wounds, until the rising sun forced them apart. Mac fully intends to kill Kirk when he gets the chance.
Opposes the Invictus utterly, viewing the covenant as the avatar of evil and unsalvageable.
René Meyer owes Mac a major boon for digging her out of a bombsite; he pretends not to think of it but keeps close track.
Sired by Venkat Haten, an Invictus Nosferatu in the Constantinople court in 1896, as an act of inter-Invictus rivalry and spite, embraced in front of his bride on their wedding bed.

Embraced on his wedding day during wartime. His Daeva bride intended to Sire him herself after consummation, but a dominate effect dropped and his eventual Nosferatu sire stepped from the shadows and embraced him instead, as inter-Invictus spite.
Constantinople (now Istanbul), in the Turkish court, 1896.

Originally Invictus; his standing was poisoned by conspiracy and he was punished with staking, torpor and imprisonment for 34 years before release in the 1950s. Now a Carthian by practicality, leading the Mammon Cult as covenant groundwork; holds the Carthians' loftier goals as lip-service.
Was dominated and forced to diablerise his own sire as a tool of his wife's family's revenge, then taken for punishment; the memory of the Amaranth was later wiped by the Mnemosyne (Keeper).
Spent his Requiem across Constantinople (1896), torpor (1896-1950), Constantinople again (1950-78), and Louisiana USA (1978-2025). The Louisiana court is famously stable with a strong Crone presence.

His Daeva wife bore a dhampir daughter, raised at arm's length by a ghoul family; the line descended all female. His great-granddaughter is the Crone Brandy LaRoux, turned by Gangrel in 1978 and sent to Sydney by the Louisiana Crones. She is the spitting image of his wife.

A portrait of his wife intended as a wedding gift, a precious keepsake that is agony to look at; and a Turkish washerwoman running a dry-cleaning operation near Macquarie Street, whose incessant complaints he treats as a lifeline to human experience and whom he quietly protects.

Feeds from his cult, who are manipulated into offering their own necks in pursuit of their own interests, almost always unseen and technically willing. He is extremely careful not to kill them, treating them as a precious resource.

Publicly in Sydney for Carthian business and to extend the Mammon cult among Australian financial elites, but is actually following his great-granddaughter, the Crone Brandy LaRoux, at a distance, whom he secretly supports and protects.
Aspires to an Enforcer role; motivated to resist internal Carthian agendas that want to 'fix' the Movement, which he prefers as the mess it is.

Holds the Masquerade as super important and views Progeny as a political tool weaponised by elites. Has true contempt for the Invictus and actively seeks separation from them; manipulates mortals as managed tools, with honey not vinegar.
Opposes the Invictus, intending to feed them lies until they destroy themselves; senses some Invictus-aligned Carthians are deeply suspicious of him.
He committed diablerie on his own sire (an Amaranth), but the memory was lifted from his head by the Mnemosyne (Keeper), who alone knows the secret. The grudge and its unresolved reasons remain.
cnr Goulborn St & Dixon St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia - Sealed off subterranean network, located about 12-18 metres below street level.
Grew up in Belfast; idolised his father, who moved from England to Ireland and joined the Royal Irish Constabulary (the 'Black and Tans').

Sired (and Embraced) by his own father, a leader within the Crimson Cavalry, as a Gangrel Paladin. The sire is now destroyed: after Kirk defected to the Lance and hunted the Cavalry, he pulverised his sire, who with his last strength commanded Kirk to diablerise him; Conrad intervened and slew the sire himself.

Joined a fledgling organisation within the Ulster Defence Association known as 'The Cavalry', aiming to protect Irish Protestants and reunify Ireland under English rule; was slowly radicalised before his Embrace.

Embraced into the Crimson Cavalry, a Gangrel paramilitary group that repeatedly violated the Tradition of Progeny; he later defected and became a Paladin of the Lancea et Sanctum (Westminster Creed), serving Inquisitor Conrad Sondergaard.

A utilitarian Embrace presented as a rite of passage. He was staring at a crucifix in the underground chapel of the Crimson Cavalry, unaware his father stood behind him with fangs bared, when he was turned; to be initiated he had burned down a Catholic monastery as a show of faith.

His resolve in the Cavalry broke when his father cajoled him to murder a pregnant Jewish refugee from the Holocaust purely for the 'racial purity' of the kine herd; distraught, he fled to London. There an Inquisitor, Conrad Sondergaard, mistook him for a werewolf suspect and recruited him.
Was made a 'Sorcerous Eunuch' by the Lancea et Sanctum.
Tracking 'Grimalkin', an assassin of the Crones (reportedly a Gangrel) dispatched to thwart the heretic-hunters of the Sanctified; his current focus alongside protecting Conrad.
His sister's mortal children still live in Ulster, now with grown children of their own. He follows them clandestinely on social media via his herd but has never made contact.

A bastard sword taken as a trophy from an Ivory Claw lupine he slew; and a Star of David pendant of the kine whose death set his life on a fresh path, for whose soul he prays and whose name he wishes he knew.
Fosters connections with young, disadvantaged men as a Christian 'Scene Queen', disguising his feeding as one-on-one prayer at small Bible studies, faith retreats and men's sheds.
His first kill was before the Embrace, during the Irish Civil War: feeding on a young IRA foot soldier, the intimacy turned sexually charged and the Beast drank deep and killed him.
Inseparable from Inquisitor Conrad for decades, viewing him as his spiritual leader and benefactor; his Sydney duties are to protect Conrad and to establish the Lance as a fixture in the city.
Wishes to ascend alongside Conrad: as Conrad becomes Bishop then Cardinal, Kirk wants to rise to the rank of Crusader at his side, serving as his Enforcer.

Came to the Traditions late, understanding them as church doctrine and being grateful his prior sins were forgiven through violence, anointing and penance. Sees mortals and half-mortals as pitiful, broken things he both despises and pities.
Has been Conrad Sondergaard's shadow and personal paladin since the night Conrad slew his father; they form a coterie of two and have often tangled with the Circle of the Crone.
Hates the Circle of the Crone, whom he calls 'witchlings'; his named enemy 'Grimalkin' is a Gangrel Crone assassin he is tracking. He also despises the Crimson Cavalry and all who share their views.
Believes he owes Conrad a life debt, though none has been officially acknowledged by either party.
He nearly diablerised his own father and sire; Conrad knows and forgave him. The weight of nearly breaking the Traditions weighs heavy on Kirk, though Conrad considers it of nearly no consequence.
Sired by Melissa, a mentor figure, now in torpor.
Embraced willingly and raised as something of a transcendence beyond the human.
Her early nights were in Italy.
Mortal family name Vaickion; Ludica avoids them as a painful reminder.
A previous lover.
Seductive.
Her first kill was in self-defence against someone, using seduction.
Seeks to be part of high vampire society and to uncover hidden knowledge about it.
Aspires to a Socialite role; wishes to assist the Invictus by holding information on others and upholding Invictus hierarchy and traditions.
Regards the Traditions as sacred laws and honours Elysium as a sanctuary; views mortals as remembrances of humanity.
The Harbour, Jones Lane, Ultimo. (Have spoken to Justice Reed to about having my safe Haven in the Habour Territory).
Embraced from English nobility under a falling meteorite, an omen taken as a sign of expected potency and role; a formal, well-attended affair for which he was groomed and prepared.

facts.note: self-referential Mnemosyne lineage; no separate named sire
facts.value: Sire and childe are one and the same: Keeper is an Elder of the Mnemosyne bloodline whose memories have merged so that he is both his own sire and his own childe; the other identity now sleeps within him.
A disgraced former high-level Invictus, he was tempted away to the Circle of the Crone by a Crone more understanding of his blood addiction, and came to worship Mnemosyne as a god.
Spent his early nights in London in the late 1700s, then a tightly held Invictus domain.
Last resided in a stable Praxis in Eastern Europe held by a Triumvirate of Scions and the Order of St Martin.
Sent far away to Sydney to stop his rampant blood-sympathies polluting other continents; dragged here with his protector/jailer Charlie Ballsack.

Spread the influence of his cult, the Remembrance of the Lidless Eye, deepen his mastery of Cruac, and recover memories lost to the Dead Zone; rebuild his reputation as a feared and respected Elder; thread the needle between Crone and the Invictus Order of St Martin. Aspires to Primogen.

Detests change and those who drive it; values something stable and unchanging to return to. Honours the Traditions reluctantly, having seen what happens when they are not observed, and always upholds Elysium as he abhors physical violence.
Feeds willingly from his Mnemosyne cult, the Remembrance of the Lidless Eye; otherwise walks the streets, clouds a chosen mortal's mind, and drinks their memories as much as their blood.

facts.sheet_value: ["She is and was always the one who grounded Henry in times of turmoil. He loved her truly once, but his vampiric obsession can never let her go.","Keeper now trains David, their shared obsession for perfection keeps Keeper focused and present.","This was the opera that started Henry's obsession and reminds him of the wonder to be found in new things."]
facts.value: His lost love, whom he keeps alive within himself and sometimes pours into a mortal who resembles her; possibly a long-worn kimono.
Grandchilde Conrad Sondergaard of the Lancea, and Anichka of the Crone.
Shares a Haven in a coterie with Charlie Ballsack and Ivana Horvat.
Rene Meyer of the Carthians.
Opposes the Carthian Movement, viewing them as short-sighted and infantile yet growing more potent each decade.
Has a relapsing blood addiction, known to Charlie, Ivana, and Anichka.
11 Bennett Road, Londonderry, NSW, Australia, 2753
Worked gig jobs in life, including as a DoorDash delivery driver.
Embraced violently after a DoorDash delivery went wrong: ran into his blood-sire in a back alley and was accidentally drained dry, then turned to right the mistake rather than left for dead.
Blood-sire Morgan Pennfold, whom he hates; not seen in about a year. His foster-sire is Aleksander Ambrose, an Invictus vampire who took him in and disciplines him.
Spent his early nights in New Zealand, quiet for both kine and kindred, until he upset Aleksander Ambrose, the one vampire in his city worth a damn.
Came to Sydney from a small demesne in Christchurch ruled by his foster-sire Aleksander Ambrose.
His mortal family is still alive and likely wondering where he is; they would not believe the truth.
Driven to win the approval of his foster-sire and prove he was worth pulling out of the gutter; sent to Sydney to connect with other Invictus and lay groundwork for Aleksander's seizure of power.
Seeks the seat of a ruler, though acknowledges he is realistically suited to be an Enforcer; covertly craves approval from Invictus royalty.

Young and unidealistic; adopts his foster-sire's stance on the Traditions and honours Elysium only to avoid embarrassing the Invictus. Privately doubts the importance of the Masquerade and finds Amaranth abhorrent.

Secretly relishes predating kine: uses knowledge of streets and back alleys to find isolated targets, lets them know he is there to sour their blood with cortisol, then overpowers them. Trying to stop draining victims dry now that others depend on him.
First kill came in his early nights after leaving his blood-sire, giving in to the Beast against warning; the act caught the attention of his foster-sire and earned his first round of discipline.
His old phone, broken when his foster-sire punished him severely for disrupting their business; a reminder of who he was and why that self deserved to be broken.
Opposes the Carthian Movement because he sees in them who he used to be and hates it.
Will not tolerate betrayal of an agreement made, nor insubordination within a consenting hierarchy.
Was open with the other Invictus that he is technically not yet part of the Covenant, and trusts them to keep it safe rather than weaponise it.
10 Aylesbury Street, Botany
Worked in corporate at the top, as an executive assistant (EA).
Knew the Embrace was coming and sought out someone willing to transform her; it was planned and noted in her diary in red pen. She returned to work as soon as able with a new vision for the company.
Turned by another EA from a sister company, himself turned many eons ago; she believes he still resides in Germany, working away.
Spent her early nights in Berlin.
Had a husband once, no children; she left him on becoming kindred lest he discover what she did. She knows which nursing home he is now in but does not visit.
Came to Sydney to run away, getting as far from Germany as possible from those she fears cannot reach her here; attends court for knowledge and protection, needing allies to survive past a century.
Wants protection and to be respectably middle-ranked, not in charge; aspires to be a Socialite.

Chose the Invictus freely for its tradition, hierarchy, oaths and organisation; holds Tradition as what separates kindred from the rabble, though reserves the right to interpret the rules to her advantage. Views humans and ghouls as tools and tradeable resources.
Targets people of no use to society, never useful ones; far more likely to cultivate willing vessels and keeps cold storage for emergencies to avoid the hassle of a hunt.
Her first kill was a loss of control after letting herself go hungry too long; she now prepares carefully so it never recurs.
A notebook she always carries; note-taking tethers her to her humanity and she feels naked without one, fighting tooth and nail to recover it if taken.
Draws the line at killing another assistant, aid or servant; they are doing their job and should be left alone.
A warrior and killer in life; fought in Vietnam in 1954 and later worked as a gun for hire in New Orleans. A cold, detached killer drawn to cults and the occult.

While investigating a cult in New Orleans he was tracked and targeted by a vampire who wanted his skills for the Carthian Movement; the Embrace was clinical and a welcomed chance to explore what he saw as his true self.
Aldric von Riven, an active senior member of the Louisiana Court and Carthian Movement, who sought Xavier's skills; provides some mentorship but is generally distant and preoccupied.
Became a killer and fixer for the Carthian Movement, which gave him purpose and an outlet for his violence over roughly 70 years in New Orleans.
Embraced and based in New Orleans; spent about 70 years there before coming to Sydney.
Parents were killed in France in WW2 when he was aged 9; he knows of no living relatives, though this does not mean none exist.

Called to Sydney by Yusuf, with whom he has worked many years; Yusuf believes there is a plot against the Carthian Movement and that current arrangements are subterfuge. Xavier comes to uphold the Movement's ideals and protect its reputation, also wanting a change after 70 years in New Orleans.
Serves as an enforcer; hopes to create a coterie of like-minded Nosferatu.

Views Traditions as necessary evils and secretly wants to try Amaranth; honours Elysium only when watched; sees humans as food and tools, ghouls as servants only. Embraces the Nosferatu ideal that other vampires should fear them.
Ambush feeder with no real lines.
His first victim as a vampire was an outright kill; it took time to learn to feed and return rather than kill, and he still relishes killing when the opportunity arises.

Juliette, daughter of his fallen Vietnam comrade and business partner Jules Laundry; Xavier has long protected and financially supported her family in New Orleans. Now in her 60s, she believes a son of Xavier's (really Xavier using Obfuscate) keeps the family safe.
Long history with Yusuf, an elder in the same covenant with whom he has frequently worked in the Carthian Movement; respects Yusuf and is in a coterie with him.
Has an inherent mistrust of the Invictus.
Will not tolerate betrayal of the Carthian Movement's ideals and purpose.
Yusuf knows about Xavier's touchstone Juliette and her extended family.
Moving from our warrens under the Harbour to the newly rediscovered Necropolis.
Albert St, Redfern
